components.title: Two-Factor Authentication Service (Duo Security)
components.description: Two-factor authentication (2FA) service (Duo Security, or equivalent such as RSA SecurID) bolting multi-factor authentication onto systems that cannot federate to the cloud Identity Provider directly — VPN remote access, SSH/RDP jump host logon, on-premises legacy applications, and local Windows/Linux logon via an authentication proxy. Supports push notification, TOTP (time-based one-time password) authenticator app codes, and WebAuthn/FIDO2 security keys as the phishing-resistant option. Complements the Identity Provider (Microsoft Entra ID) service, which handles MFA for cloud/SSO-federated applications, and the Privileged Access Management platform, which handles MFA for brokered privileged sessions.
components.purpose: Provide multi-factor authentication for systems and access paths that are not covered by the cloud Identity Provider's Conditional Access policies, so that no user-facing or administrative logon path in the environment is protected by a password alone.

props.name: vendor
props.value: Cisco Duo Security / RSA SecurID / equivalent

props.name: deployment-model
props.value: Cloud-hosted 2FA service with an on-premises Authentication Proxy for LDAP/RADIUS-integrated systems (VPN concentrator, SSH bastion, RDP gateway)

props.name: factors-supported
props.value: Push notification (Duo Mobile), TOTP authenticator app codes, WebAuthn/FIDO2 security keys, SMS/phone callback (fallback only, discouraged)

props.name: phishing-resistant-option
props.value: WebAuthn/FIDO2 security key enrolment available and preferred for privileged accounts

props.name: integration-points
props.value: VPN Remote Access (RADIUS), SSH bastion/jump host (PAM module), RDP Gateway, on-premises legacy web applications (RADIUS/LDAP proxy)

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — 2FA service admin console and push notification API

protocols.name: radius
protocols.title: RADIUS — Authentication proxy integration with VPN concentrator, SSH bastion, RDP gateway

protocols.name: ldaps
protocols.title: LDAPS — Authentication proxy lookups against Active Directory for username/primary-factor validation

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — Authentication event forwarding to SIEM

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for the Two-Factor Authentication Service (Duo Security).

implemented-requirements.description: Multi-factor authentication is enforced for unprivileged users authenticating to systems and access paths covered by this 2FA service — VPN remote access, SSH/RDP jump host logon, and on-premises legacy applications — closing the gap for access paths that cannot federate to the cloud Identity Provider's Conditional Access policies.

Multi-factor authentication is enforced for privileged users authenticating via the access paths covered by this 2FA service, with WebAuthn/FIDO2 security key enrolment required (rather than push notification or TOTP) for privileged accounts.

The second factor enforced by this service is something the user has (a registered mobile device running Duo Mobile, a hardware security key, or a TOTP authenticator app) combined with something the user knows (their primary directory password), satisfying the required combination of authentication factors.

For privileged and administrative accounts, multi-factor authentication enforced by this service is phishing-resistant, using WebAuthn/FIDO2 security key enrolment rather than push notification, TOTP, or SMS-based factors, which remain susceptible to MFA-fatigue and real-time phishing relay attacks.

Where a user account has enrolled a phishing-resistant WebAuthn/FIDO2 security key, non-phishing-resistant factors (SMS, phone callback) are disabled for that account so a compromised phone number cannot be used to bypass the stronger factor.

Legacy authentication paths that do not support multi-factor authentication are identified and either integrated with this 2FA service via the Authentication Proxy (RADIUS/LDAP) or decommissioned — no production access path is permitted to bypass MFA enforcement.

Successful and unsuccessful multi-factor authentication events (push approvals/denials, TOTP failures, fallback SMS usage) are centrally logged by the 2FA service and forwarded to the SIEM, supporting detection of MFA-fatigue attacks and credential-stuffing campaigns.
Administrators of the 2FA service platform itself are assigned dedicated privileged accounts, separate from their standard user accounts, used solely for 2FA policy and integration administration.
